highlights: Built a global skill architecture with reusable SKILL.md contracts to standardize agent behavior across domains.
Implemented routing, context isolation, memory layers, and tool orchestration patterns for consistent multi-agent execution.
Added operational guardrails for verification-first delivery, non-destructive git handling, and recoverable workflows.
impact: Improved reliability and output consistency across coding, research, and operations workflows while reducing time to deploy new agent capabilities.
one_liner: Global AI harness architecture for reusable skills, multi-agent orchestration, memory continuity, and production-safe execution.
problem: As agent usage expanded, project-by-project prompt logic and ad hoc tooling created drift in quality, reliability, and maintainability.
solution: Designed a shared harness layer that unifies architecture standards, skills, memory, routing, and execution constraints into one operating model.
title: AI Harness Engineering System
visibility_note: Core repository is private. This entry summarizes architecture and working approach without exposing proprietary source.
